name: Thirty-Year Time Wheel
description: Set a real calendar with three concentric angular controls while managing physical release momentum and braking.
natural_language: Wind every ring to the target date. Stop all release momentum before you LOCK.

metadata.notes: Pointer angle drives real detents on three calendar components; fast release creates discrete decaying momentum that can be caught by the brake, but pass depends on the calendar outcome rather than a demonstration quota.
